channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, May 20th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9938, (Wed, May 20th)
Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector
item.description: Verizon’s 2026 DBIR finds vulnerability exploitation has overtaken credential abuse as the leading breach vector, as AI accelerates attacks, patching delays worsen, and ransomware and third-party compromises continue...

item.title: Max-severity flaw in ChromaDB for AI apps allows server hijacking
item.description: A max-severity vulnerability in the latest Python FastAPI version of the ChromaDB project allows unauthenticated attackers to run arbitrary code on exposed servers. [...]

item.title: Stealer Spoofs Google, Microsoft & Apple, Then Backdoors macOS
item.description: The SHub Reaper stealer, which hides behind fake WeChat and Miro installers, marks a shift from ClickFix social engineering to Apple script-based execution.

item.title: Microsoft dismantled malware-signing network Fox Tempest
item.description: Microsoft disrupted Fox Tempest, a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) that allowed attackers to sign malware with fake trusted certificates. Microsoft said it disrupted a cybercrime operation run by a threat actor n...

item.title: Legacy Windows Tool MSHTA Fuels Surge in Silent Malware Attacks
item.description: Attackers are increasingly abusing Microsoft’s decades-old MSHTA utility to stealthily deliver stealers, loaders, and persistent malware through phishing, fake software downloads, and LOLBIN-based attack chains. The p...

item.title: Webinar: The hidden bottlenecks in network incident response
item.description: IT teams are increasingly overwhelmed by alerts from disconnected systems, forcing responders to manually coordinate investigations during network incidents. This webinar explores how automation and AI-assisted workfl...

item.title: PoC Released for DirtyDecrypt Linux Kernel Vulnerability
item.description: Patched in April, the underlying vulnerability allows local attackers to elevate their privileges to root. The post PoC Released for DirtyDecrypt Linux Kernel Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway Vulnerabilities Enable RCE and Mail Traffic Access
item.description: Critical security vulnerabilities have been disclosed in SEPPMail Secure E-Mail Gateway, an enterprise-grade email security solution, that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution and enable an attacker to...

item.title: How We Got a CISA GitHub Leak Taken Down in Under a Day
item.description: On May 14, GitGuardian found a public GitHub repository called "Private-CISA" — 844 MB of plain-text passwords, AWS tokens, and Entra ID SAML certificates belonging to CISA, exposed since November 2025. Some credentia...

item.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Pushes Malicious AntV npm Packages via Compromised Maintainer Account
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a fresh software supply chain attack campaign that has compromised various npm packages associated with the @antv ecosystem as part of the ongoing Mini Shai-Hulud attack wave....

item.title: Verizon DBIR: Enterprises Face a Dangerous Vulnerability Glut
item.description: Verizon's "2026 Data Breach Investigations Report" ("DBIR") finds that exploits are now involved in 31% of initial access for breaches, while patching lags too far behind the bad guys.

item.title: Windows Zero-Day Barrage Continues After Patch Tuesday
item.description: YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma add to the growing list of vulnerabilities a security researcher disclosed over the past six weeks.

item.title: FBI: Americans lost over $388 million to scams using crypto ATMs in 2025
item.description: The FBI says Americans have lost over $388 million last year to scams using cryptocurrency kiosks, also known as crypto ATMs or Bitcoin ATMs. [...]

item.title: Drupal is rolling out an emergency security update on May 20. You cannot miss it
item.description: Drupal Is Pushing an Emergency Security Update Tomorrow. If You Run a Drupal Site, This Is Not One to Miss. Something significant is coming out of the Drupal project tomorrow, and the way the announcement is worded sh...

item.title: Microsoft Self-Service Password Reset abused in Azure data theft attacks
item.description: A threat actor targeting Microsoft 365 and Azure production environments is stealing data in attacks that abuse legitimate applications and administration features. [...]

item.title: America's top cyber-defense agency left a GitHub repo open with with passwords, keys, tokens – and incredibly obvious filenames
item.description: I wonder what's in 'external-secret-repo-creds.yaml' and 'AWS-Workspace-Firefox-Passwords.csv'?

item.title: Clear your calendar, Drupal user: You have a critically urgent patch to install
item.description: The org’s staying mum on the details, but Wednesday’s fixes reach back to unsupported 8.9 branches

item.title: Trapdoor Android Ad Fraud Scheme Hit 659 Million Daily Bid Requests Using 455 Apps
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new ad fraud and malvertising operation dubbed Trapdoor targeting Android device users. The activity, per HUMAN's Satori Threat Intelligence and Research Team, enc...

item.title: Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation
item.description: Drupal says attackers may develop an exploit for the vulnerability within hours or days. The post Drupal to Patch Highly Critical Vulnerability at Risk of Quick Exploitation appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Governing infrastructure as code using pattern-based policy as code
item.description: Organizations often struggle to enforce security and compliance requirements consistently across their cloud infrastructure. In one environment, a workload might be deployed in an AWS Region that was never approved fo...

item.title: TP-Link, Photoshop, OpenVPN, Norton VPN vulnerabilities
item.description: Cisco Talos’ Vulnerability Discovery & Research team recently disclosed eight vulnerabilities in TP-Link, and one each in Adobe Photoshop, OpenVPN, and Gen Digital's Norton VPN. The vulnerabilities mentioned in this b...

item.title: Rapid7’s 2026 Global Cybersecurity Summit: Key Takeaways for Security Leaders
item.description: Security teams are working in an environment where speed, scale, and complexity are all increasing at the same time. Across the Rapid7 2026 Global Cybersecurity Summit , the focus was not just on how the threat landsc...

item.title: 7-Eleven confirms data breach claimed by the ShinyHunters gang
item.description: Convenience store chain giant 7-Eleven confirmed that its systems were breached in a cyberattack claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion group last month. [...]

item.title: Looking Back, Looking Forward: Digesting a Dynamic Bouillabaisse of Cyber Evolution
item.description: Dark Reading editors reflect on two decades of dramatic change — from perimeter defense to assume-breach strategies — and warn that while AI, cloud, and COVID-19 have transformed the threat landscape, organizations ar...

item.title: Introducing Runtime Threat Detection for Google Cloud Run
item.description: Wiz Runtime Sensor support for Google Cloud Run Containers is now generally available, giving teams real-time threat detection and response for their serverless container workloads.

item.title: PureLogs infostealer is stealing credentials worldwide
item.description: A phishing campaign is smuggling the powerful PureLogs information stealer onto targets’ Windows machines by hiding encrypted malicious payloads inside cat photos, Fortinet researchers discovered. The attack The attac...

item.title: Selector extends AI-driven observability into multi-cloud environments
item.description: Selector has announced the expansion of its platform with AI-powered multi-cloud observability capabilities. The extension of Selector’s AI-driven observability approach into multi-cloud environments enables organizat...

item.title: Announcing Claude Managed Agents on Cloudflare
item.description: Cloudflare has integrated with Anthropic's Claude Managed Agents to provide a fast, isolated execution environment for autonomous code delivery. This means builders can scale agent workflows globally while strictly co...

item.title: The AI Data You Forgot to Lock: How Exposed Vector Databases Put Organizations at Risk
item.description: The AI revolution has brought a new class of infrastructure into the mainstream: vector databases. Tools like Weaviate, Milvus, and ChromaDB have become essential building blocks for AI-powered applications from retri...

item.title: LaunchDarkly adds real-time controls for AI agents in production
item.description: LaunchDarkly has launched AgentControl, a new solution that gives software teams real-time control over AI agents in production. With AgentControl, teams can change how an agent behaves at runtime without redeploying...

item.title: Canonical ships Ubuntu Core 26 with 15 years of security maintenance
item.description: Operators of industrial sensors, edge AI controllers, and connected medical equipment now have a refreshed long-term Linux option for fleets that must stay patched for more than a decade. Canonical released Ubuntu Cor...

item.title: AI Raises the Bar on Vulnerability Awareness and Secure-by-Design Software
item.description: AI-powered vulnerability scanning leaves no excuse for unpatched bugs as the EU Cyber Resilience Act pushes firms toward secure-by-design software

item.title: The end of unencrypted Discord calls is here
item.description: Discord has protected voice and video calls in DMs, group DMs, voice channels, and Go Live streams with end-to-end encryption (E2EE) by default. The company began experimenting with E2EE for voice and video in 2023, s...

item.title: The New Phishing Click: How OAuth Consent Bypasses MFA
item.description: In February 2026, a phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform called EvilTokens went live. Within five weeks, it had compromised more than 340 Microsoft 365 organizations across five countries. The targets of the platfor...

item.title: Drupal to Release Urgent Core Security Updates on May 20, Sites Told to Prepare
item.description: Drupal has issued an alert stating that it intends to release a "core security release" for all supported branches on May 20, 2026, from 5-9 p.m. UTC. "The Drupal Security Team urges you to reserve time for core updat...

item.title: Agentic AI Accelerates Software Builds and Mobile App Attacks
item.description: Digital.ai data reveals 87% of apps were attacked over the past year

item.title: B1ack’s Stash Marketplace Gives Away 4.6 Million Stolen Credit Cards
item.description: The stolen credit card data was released as a free download, allegedly in response to seller misconduct. The post B1ack’s Stash Marketplace Gives Away 4.6 Million Stolen Credit Cards appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Cyber Resilience is the New Business Continuity Plan
item.description: The organizations best prepared to face disruption are those that align security, continuity and risk management around what the business cannot afford to lose. The post Cyber Resilience is the New Business Continuity...

item.title: Microsoft confirms patching issues in restricted Windows networks
item.description: Microsoft says customers in restricted network environments may encounter Windows Update failures after installing the January 2026 optional non-security preview updates. [...]

item.title: Laurie Anderson Is Quoting Me
item.description: Not by name, but Laurie Anderson quotes me in one of the tracks of her new album: My favorite quote is from a cryptologist who said “If you think technology will solve your problems, you don’t understand technology an...

item.title: 201 Arrested in Crackdown on Cybercrime in Middle East, North Africa
item.description: The 13-country effort, named Operation Ramz, targeted cyber threats in the Middle East and North Africa region. The post 201 Arrested in Crackdown on Cybercrime in Middle East, North Africa appeared first on SecurityW...

item.title: From PDB strings to MaaS: Tracking a commodity BadIIS ecosystem used by Chinese-speaking threat
item.description: Cisco Talos has uncovered a BadIIS variant — identifiable by its embedded "demo.pdb" strings — that functions as commodity malware, likely sold or shared among multiple Chinese-speaking cyber crime groups operating un...

item.title: Grafana Labs Confirms Hackers Stole Source Code
item.description: Open source tool maker Grafana says hackers stole codebase via GitHub breach

item.title: The Worm That Keeps on Digging: TeamPCP Hits @antv in Latest Wave
item.description: Multi-ecosystem supply chain compromise by TeamPCP targets GitHub, NPM, and VSCode to steal credentials and establish persistence.

item.title: Hackers Bypass Security Tools to Target Users Directly
item.description: Bridewell report calls out emergence of “fix-style” attacks

item.title: Compromised Nx Console 18.95.0 Targeted VS Code Developers with Credential Stealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a compromised version of the Nx Console extension that was published to the Microsoft Visual Studio Code (VS Code) Marketplace. The extension in question is rwl.angular-console (...

item.title: Babel Street targets AI-driven threats with new agentic investigation capabilities
item.description: Babel Street has launched Insights Investigator, a new agentic capability that puts tradecraft-trained AI agents at the front edge of investigative work while ensuring analysts remain in control of scope, logic, and o...

item.title: iProov brings identity verification to video meetings to reduce fraud risks
item.description: iProov has launched iProov Verified Meetings, a new solution that enables organizations to verify the identity of video call participants without adding friction to the user experience. Video meetings have become a tr...

item.title: Shai-Hulud worm copycats emerge after source code leak
item.description: Shai-Hulud worm copycats are already attacking NPM developers after its source code leaked, enabling fast supply chain exploitation. The first copycats of the Shai-Hulud worm have already started showing up online, on...

item.title: Egnyte unveils Email Capture and AI features to unify fragmented data
item.description: Egnyte has announced a new set of capabilities designed to consolidate fragmented knowledge. Email Capture centralizes critical communications and attachments from siloed inboxes into the Egnyte folder structure, assi...

item.title: Critical Vulnerability Exposes Industrial Robot Fleets to Hacking
item.description: The vulnerability, CVE-2026-8153, affects Universal Robots PolyScope 5 and it can be exploited for OS command injection. The post Critical Vulnerability Exposes Industrial Robot Fleets to Hacking appeared first on Sec...

item.title: Public Instagram posts provide raw material for AI phishing campaigns
item.description: A handful of public Instagram posts can give attackers enough material to generate convincing phishing emails with GenAI. Research from the University of Texas at Arlington and Louisiana State University showed how pu...

item.title: Earbud sensors can authenticate users by their heartbeat, study finds
item.description: Researchers built a continuous authentication system called AccLock that identifies a wearer by the tiny vibrations a heartbeat makes inside the ear canal. The signal comes from an accelerometer of the kind already si...

item.title: AI infrastructure is cracking under sovereignty demands
item.description: AI deployments are moving into environments with tighter controls around data, infrastructure, and system operations. Organizations are building AI systems across multiple providers, platforms, and computing environme...

item.title: Cybersecurity jobs available right now: May 19, 2026
item.description: CISO DataFence | Israel | Hybrid – No longer accepting applications As a CISO, you will develop security roadmaps, compliance plans, risk registers, policies, and control implementation plans while leading audit and r...

item.title: Agentic AI Tooling: Why Runtime Security Is the Missing Layer
item.description: A developer asks an AI coding agent to refactor a microservice. Within seconds, the agent opens source files, executes shell commands, calls external APIs, and modifies cloud configuration. No human approves each step...

item.title: Building for the AI era: Chainguard partners with Endor Labs
item.description: Chainguard and Endor Labs help teams build securely at AI speed with source-built artifacts, exploitability analysis, and fewer vulnerabilities to triage.

item.title: Chaotic Eclipse discloses MiniPlasma zero-day, suggesting a missing or undone 2020 Windows security fix
item.description: MiniPlasma: a Windows SYSTEM privilege escalation believed patched in 2020 (CVE-2020-17103) is still fully working on every patched Windows 11. Once again, security researcher Chaotic Eclipse has released a proof-of-c...

item.title: 'Claw Chain' Vulnerabilities Threaten OpenClaw Deployments
item.description: The now-patched vulnerabilities in the rapidly growing AI agent framework allow attackers to steal credentials, escalate privileges, and maintain persistence.

item.title: Is 2026 the Year AI Bills of Materials Get Real?
item.description: Understanding AI BOMs and where they fit into risk management for artificial intelligence.

item.title: TanStack weighs invitation-only pull requests after supply chain attack
item.description: Shai-Hulud worm exploited GitHub Actions misconfiguration to poison shared cache, now project weighing nuclear option on unsolicited contributions

item.title: Security Researchers Find 47 Zero-Days at Pwn2Own Berlin
item.description: The research community was awarded $1.3m as it found dozens of novel vulnerabilities at Pwn2Own Berlin

item.title: Experts warn of active exploitation of critical NGINX flaw CVE-2026-42945
item.description: A critical NGINX flaw (CVE-2026-42945) is actively exploited, allowing crashes or possible code execution via malicious HTTP requests. A critical vulnerability in NGINX Plus and NGINX Open, tracked as CVE-2026-42945 (...

item.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Activity Through 2026-05-17, (Mon, May 18th)
item.description: Since the last update, the TeamPCP supply chain campaign produced its loudest stretch since the March Trivy disclosure: an officially confirmed Checkmarx Jenkins plugin compromise and a new self-spreading Mini Shai-Hu...

item.title: Microsoft Exchange Zero-Day Under Attack, No Patch Available
item.description: CVE-2026-42897 stems from a cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability and can allow an attacker to compromise Outlook Web Access (OWA) mailboxes.

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and...

item.title: Vulnerability Summary for the Week of May 11, 2026
item.description: High Vulnerabilities Primary Vendor -- Product Description Published CVSS Score Source Info Patch Info acl--ACL Analytics ACL Analytics versions 11.x through 13.0.0.579 contain an arbitrary code execution vulnerabilit...

item.title: Fuel Tank Breaches Expand Scope of Iran's Cyber Offensive
item.description: Security experts have long warned that insecure automatic tank gauge (ATG) systems exposed on the Internet can be tampered with by threat actors.

item.title: Boulevard of Broken Dreams: 2 Decades of Cyber Fails
item.description: From the MGM and Caesars fiasco and MOVEit's patch nightmare to epic business blunders and the jaded reality of living in a post-breach world, Dark Reading looks back at the mistakes, miscalculations, systemic failure...

item.title: AI is drowning software maintainers in junk security reports
item.description: AI-assisted vulnerability research has exploded, unleashing a firehose of low-quality reports on overworked software maintainers who are wasting hours sifting through noise instead of fixing real problems. Linus Torva...

item.title: Game over for 74 suspected scammers after Dutch cops plastered their faces on billboards
item.description: The Dutch police’s Game Over?! campaign, which publicly displays images of suspected fraudsters to encourage self-surrenders and gather public tips, is proving successful, with the identities of 74 of the 100 suspects...

item.title: GenAI Risks in Cloud Environments: What Security Teams Are Actually Missing in 2026
item.description: Key Takeaways Most cloud security programs were not built for generative AI workloads. The IAM policies, network segmentation rules, and misconfiguration checks that worked for a three-tier web application in 2021 lea...

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Exchange 0-Day, npm Worm, Fake AI Repo, Cisco Exploit and More
item.description: Monday opens with a trust problem. A mail server flaw is under active use. A network control system was targeted. Trusted packages were poisoned. A fake model page pushed a stealer. Then came the familiar ransom claim...

item.title: The Infosecurity Europe Cyber Startup Competition: Meet the Finalists
item.description: New for 2026, the Infosecurity Europe Startup competition will see five finalists pitch their ideas in front of a live audience, including senior industry leaders, investors and buyers

item.title: Public Amazon bucket leaks sensitive guest data from Japanese hotel platform Tabiq
item.description: A hotel check-in system exposed over 1 million passports, IDs, and selfies online due to a misconfigured cloud storage bucket. A security lapse in the Reqrea’s Tabiq hotel check-in system exposed over 1 million passpo...

item.title: NGINX Rift attackers waste no time targeting exposed servers
item.description: Researchers say 18-year-old flaw already being probed and exploited just days after disclosure

item.title: Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches
item.description: Several healthcare data breaches impacting hundreds of thousands and even millions were added to the HHS tracker. The post Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: SmartBear expands ReadyAPI with AI-powered API testing capabilities
item.description: SmartBear has announced ReadyAPI’s new AI test generation capability that accelerates API testing by up to 80% while giving teams control to enable or disable AI. While competitors focus on speed alone, ReadyAPI’s AI...

item.title: Poland directs officials to ditch Signal in favor of 'secure' state-developed alternative
item.description: Shift comes amid mounting reports of successful social engineering attacks targeting higher-ups in government

item.title: Developer Workstations Are Now Part of the Software Supply Chain
item.description: Supply chain attackers are not only trying to slip malicious code into trusted software. They are trying to steal the access that makes trusted software possible. Recently, three separate campaigns hit npm, PyPI, and...

item.title: Zero-Day Exploit Against Windows BitLocker
item.description: It’s nasty , but it requires physical access to the computer: The exploit, named YellowKey, was published earlier this week by a researcher who goes by the alias Nightmare-Eclipse. It reliably bypasses default Windows...

item.title: From Cryptographic Blind Spots to Post-Quantum Agility: Introducing Wiz for PQC Readiness
item.description: Eliminate cryptographic blind spots and neutralize legacy debt with an integrated cryptographic asset inventory. Identify risks across code, cloud, and runtime, using the Wiz Security Graph to prioritize migration and...

item.title: NCSC Publishes Guidance on Securing Agentic AI Use
item.description: The UK’s National Cyber Security Centre is helping organizations to understand agentic AI security risks

item.title: Project Glasswing: what Mythos showed us
item.description: In recent weeks, we pointed Mythos and other security-focused LLMs at live code across critical parts of our infrastructure. We share what we observed, the models’ strengths and weaknesses, and what the work around th...

item.title: Mozilla warns UK: Breaking VPNs will not magically fix Britain's age-check mess
item.description: Firefox maker says the tools are basic security infrastructure, not teenage contraband

item.title: Bank of England, FCA and Treasury Raise Alarm Over Frontier AI
item.description: The UK’s financial authorities have set expectations for the sector on cybersecurity and operational resilience

item.title: Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins
item.description: The flaw leads to denial-of-service on default configurations and to remote code execution if ASLR is disabled. The post Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Pre-Stuxnet Fast16 Malware Tampered with Nuclear Weapons Simulations
item.description: A new analysis of the Lua-based fast16 malware has confirmed that it was a cyber sabotage tool designed to tamper with nuclear weapons testing simulations. According to Broadcom-owned Symantec and Carbon Black teams,...

item.title: The AI backdoor your security stack is not built to see
item.description: Enterprises deploying LLMs have spent the past two years building defenses around a reasonable assumption: malicious behavior leaves a trace in the input. Scan for suspicious tokens, filter unusual characters, watch f...

item.title: Lyrie: Open-source autonomous pentesting agent
item.description: Penetration testing has usually required weeks of manual work, specialized tooling, and teams with narrow skill sets. Lyrie, an open-source autonomous security agent built by OTT Cybersecurity, compresses that process...

item.title: AI shrinks vulnerability exploitation window to hours
item.description: Time has become organizations’ biggest vulnerability because the gap between vulnerability discovery and exploitation has narrowed to hours, according to Synack’s 2026 State of Vulnerabilities Report. Total vulnerabil...

item.title: Product showcase: McAfee + ChatGPT integration turns doubt into a scam check
item.description: McAfee + ChatGPT integration brings real-time scam detection in conversations and gives users an easier way to verify suspicious content before clicking or responding. It is available to anyone, without requiring a Mc...

item.title: When ransomware hits, confidence doesn’t restore endpoints
item.description: Ransomware, supply chain vulnerabilities, insider threats, compliance failures, and software disruptions remain major concerns for security leaders, according to The Ransomware Reality: Zero Days to Recover report by...

item.title: Can Laws Stop Deepfakes? South Korea Aims to Find Out
item.description: South Korea's local elections next month will be a test bed for how effective regulations might be to stymie the flow of deepfakes.

item.title: The Boring Stuff is Dangerous Now
item.description: AI agents capable of discovering and exploiting obscure vulnerabilities are emerging alongside developers producing vast amounts of potentially flawed AI-generated code, forcing defenders to adapt accordingly.

item.title: Pathfinding Labs: Deploy, test, and learn from 100+ intentionally vulnerable AWS environments
item.description: Introducing Pathfinding Labs, a collection of intentionally vulnerable AWS environments for red teamers and blue teamers to deploy, exploit, and use for detection validation.

item.title: SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 97
item.description: Security Affairs Malware newsletter includes a collection of the best articles and research on malware in the international landscape Malware Newsletter JDownloader site hacked to replace installers with Python RAT ma...

item.title: Grafana GitHub Token Breach Led to Codebase Download and Extortion Attempt
item.description: Grafana has disclosed that an "unauthorized party" obtained a token that granted them the ability to access the company's GitHub environment and download its codebase. "Our investigation has determined that no custome...

item.title: Linus Torvalds says AI-powered bug hunters have made Linux security mailing list ‘almost entirely unmanageable’
item.description: Multiple researchers using the same tools to find the same bugs are creating ‘unnecessary pain and pointless work’

item.title: Debian 13.5 point release lands with security fixes, bug patches
item.description: Debian 13.5 is the fifth point release for the stable distribution “trixie.” The update folds in roughly 100 Debian Security Advisories and corrections for more than 130 source packages, covering everything from the L...

item.title: Tycoon2FA hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via device-code phishing
item.description: The Tycoon2FA phishing kit now supports device-code phishing attacks and abuses Trustifi click-tracking URLs to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts. [...]

item.title: Russian hackers turn Kazuar backdoor into modular P2P botnet
item.description: The Russian hacker group Secret Blizzard has developed its long-running Kazuar backdoor into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet designed for long-term persistence, stealth, and data collection. [...]

item.title: PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability
item.description: Introduced in 2008, the critical-severity security defect was patched this week in NGINX Plus and NGINX open source. The post PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Bigfin Squid
item.description: Article about the bigfin squid. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Bypassing On-Camera Age-Verification Checks
item.description: Some AI-based video age-verification checks can be fooled with a fake mustache .

item.title: What 45 Days of Watching Your Own Tools Will Tell You About Your Real Attack Surface
item.description: In Your Biggest Security Risk Isn't Malware — It's What You Already Trust, we made a simple argument: the most dangerous activity inside most organizations no longer looks like an attack. It looks like administration....

item.title: China-Linked Hackers Deploy New TencShell Malware Against Global Manufacturer
item.description: A suspected China-linked threat actor targeted the Indian branch of a global manufacturer leveraging an open source offensive toolkit

item.title: [Guest Diary] New Malware Libraries means New Signatures, (Fri, May 15th)
Taiwan Bullet Train Hack Highlights Cybersecurity Gaps in Rail Systems
item.description: A Taiwanese student experimenting with software-defined radio technology shut down three bullet trains for nearly an hour, leading to an anti-terrorism response.

item.title: Congress Puts Heat on Instructure After Canvas Outage
item.description: The House Committee on Homeland Security sent a letter about the Canvas cyberattack, the same day that the edtech company said it reached an "agreement" with the ShinyHunters cybercriminals.

item.title: Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day, the Sixth Exploited in 2026
item.description: The zero-day, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, has been exploited in targeted attacks by a sophisticated threat actor identified as UAT-8616. The post Cisco Patches Another SD-WAN Zero-Day, the Sixth Exploited in 2026 appea...

item.title: Avada Builder WordPress plugin flaws allow site credential theft
item.description: Two vulnerabilities in the Avada Builder plugin for WordPress, with an estimated one million active installations, allow hackers to read arbitrary files and extract sensitive information from the database. [...]

item.title: Microsoft Exchange, Windows 11 hacked on second day of Pwn2Own
item.description: ​During the second day of Pwn2Own Berlin 2026, competitors collected $385,750 in cash awards after exploiting 15 unique zero-day vulnerabilities in multiple products, including Windows 11, Microsoft Exchange, and Red...

item.title: Metasploit Wrap-Up 05/15/2026
item.description: Weaponizing a text editor for fun and profit Gather round, dear readers, because today, we (by we, we mean @h00die) dropped the ultimate persistence mechanism: Vim plugin persistence. And honestly, calling it "persist...

item.title: The AWS AI Security Framework: Securing AI with the right controls, at the right layers, at the right phases
item.description: TL;DR for busy executives The AWS AI Security Framework helps security leaders move fast and stay secure with AI. Security compounds from day 1 as workloads evolve from prototype to production to scale. Assess first....

item.title: Linux kernel vulnerability enables local theft of SSH host keys and /etc/shadow
item.description: Executive Summary A high-impact Linux kernel vulnerability, currently without a verified public CVE or CVSS score, was disclosed affecting kernels prior to commit 31e62c2e. The issue allows a local unprivileged attack...

item.title: Welcome to BlackFile: Inside a Vishing Extortion Operation
item.description: Written by: Austin Larsen, Tyler McLellan, Genevieve Stark, Dan Ebreo Introduction Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) has continued to track an expansive extortion campaign by UNC6671, a threat actor operating un...

item.title: In Other News: Big Tech vs Canada Encryption Bill, Cisco’s Free AI Security Spec, Audi App Flaws
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Nvidia cloud gaming data breach, Android 17 security upgrades, FBI warning after ShinyHunters hacks Canvas. The post In Other News: Big Tech vs Canada...

item.title: Gremlin Stealer Evolves into Modular Threat with Advanced Evasion Capabilities
item.description: A new Gremlin stealer variant has evolved into a modular toolkit with advanced evasion and data theft capabilities, according to new Unit 42 research
